revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:今尾神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Imao jinja2008-2.jpg |name=Imao Shrine |location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|lt=Kaizu|1=Kaizu}}{{ill|Hirata-cho|ja|平田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata-cho|1=namedachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483353}} Imao 3055-8 |Latitude=35|Latitude Minutes=15|Latitude Seconds=07|N(北緯) or S(南緯)=N |Longitude=136|Longitude Minutes=37|Longitude Seconds=31|E(東経) or W(西経)=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Gifu |deity={{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724|1=Toyouke-hime}} |type=[[Gōsha|Former Gō-sha]] and [[Kinpei-sha|Kinpei-sha]] |consecration_year=Keichō 19 (1614) |Main Hall= |Other names= |Pilgrimage Stations= |Festivals=October 18 (festival) |Rituals=Spring Grand Festival, {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} (Lunar Calendar January 17) |prefecture={{ill|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|1=Kaizu}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.25194444444445|N|136.62527777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Imao Shrine''' (今尾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|lt=Kaizu|1=Kaizu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. Its Spring Grand Festival, also known as "Imao Sagichō Festival," features a large-scale {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} and has been designated an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author= |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/kaizusi/imao.html |title=今尾左義長 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-10-16}}</ref>
== Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime no Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (豊受姫大神)
; Enshrined deity * {{ill|Amatsu Ōkami|ja|天津大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == * In Keichō 19 (1614), {{ill|Toyouke-hime no Ōkami|ja|豊受姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} was transferred from the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} to the shrine grounds in Imao, establishing the shrine as Shinmei Shrine. * In Meiji 29 (1896), the Ujigami (tutelary deity) shrine of Amatsu Ōkami, destroyed during a flood of the Nagara and Ibi rivers, was merged with Shinmei Shrine after its sacred objects were safely relocated. * In Shōwa 3 (1928), Amatsu Shrine was officially merged with Shinmei Shrine, and the name was changed to Imao Shrine. * In Shōwa 9 (1934), the shrine buildings were reconstructed.
== Sub-Shrines == * Imao Gokoku Shrine – consecration_year in Shōwa 6 (1931) * Sarutahiko Shrine * Sorei Shrine – Enshrines past rulers of Imao Domain, including {{ill|Tadatoshi Takekoshi|ja|竹腰正信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadatoshi Takekoshi|1=Takenokoshi Masanobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11599411}} * Hirata Shrine – Dedicated to {{ill|Hirata Yukie|en|Hirata Yukie|qid=Q11483406|lt=Hirata Yuzuru|1=Hirata Yukie}} and 84 retainers of the Satsuma Domain who perished during the {{ill|1754 Hōreki River incident|en|1754 Hōreki River incident|qid=Q4552908|lt=Hōreki River Improvement Incident|1=1754 Hōreki River incident}} * Inari Shrine * Imao Akiba Shrine (external site) – Sacred travel destination of Imao Shrine
== Festivals == === Imao Sagichō Festival === [[File:Imao Sagicyo2008-09.jpg|thumb|200px|2008 Sagichō Festival at Imao Akiba Shrine ([[:commons:Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]]
)]] * Held on Lunar Calendar January 17, this {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} features drum-shaped bamboo mikoshi (portable shrines) constructed by parishioners. Each mikoshi is approximately 6 meters tall, 6 meters in circumference, and weighs about 2 tons, using over 1,000 bamboo stalks. These mikoshi are decorated with divine talismans from Imao Shrine and Imao Akiba Shrine, adorned with prawn ornaments and banners inscribed with wishes for harmony, prosperity, and longevity. * Early in the morning, rituals are conducted at Imao Shrine before parishioners carry the mikoshi to Imao Akiba Shrine, where it is set alight using a flame passed down by the shrine priest. Participants run around the burning mikoshi dressed in undergarments and white tabi socks. Once 70% of the structure is burned, it is felled in the direction of the year's {{ill|Eho|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eho|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}}, foretelling fortune or misfortune. Eating mochi roasted over the remnants is believed to ensure good health. * The Sagichō predates the creation of Imao Akiba Shrine in Meiwa 4 (1767). A fire in Ansei 5 (1858) led to the festival's cancellation by Takekoshi Masatomi, the ninth lord of Imao Domain. The following year, the festival was reinstated as a fire-prevention ritual after the domain experienced a catastrophic fire during the suspension.
== location == ; Address * Imao 3055-8, {{ill|Hirata-cho|ja|平田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata-cho|1=namedachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483353}}, {{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|lt=Kaizu|1=Kaizu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}
; Access * Nearest Bus Stop: Meihan Kintetsu Bus, Kaizu Line, "Imao" stop, approximately 8 minutes on foot (650 meters north)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Imao Akiba Shrine|ja|今尾秋葉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Akiba Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|lt=Kaizu|1=Kaizu}}
== References == === Citations ===
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=643&shrname=%E2%98%85%E4%BB%8A%E5%B0%BE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Imao Shrine - Gifu Shrine Office] * [http://www.ogaki-tv.ne.jp/~max21/sagityo/index.html Imao Sagichō - The Festival of Wonders]
